tweets.title: PM Modi wishes Trump on 250th Independence anniversary of US.
tweets.content:
PM Modi wishes Trump on 250th Independence anniversary of US.

Narendra Modi (@narendramodi)

On behalf of 1.4 billion Indians, I extend my warmest congratulations to President Trump and the people of the United States on the historic 250th anniversary of your Independence.

India and the United States share more than a strategic partnership. Our shared belief in democracy, rule of law and the limitless potential of our people make our friendship a force for global good.

May the next 250 years bring even greater prosperity, peace and progress for America and take the India-US partnership to new heights.

@POTUS

@realDonaldTrump

— https://nitter.net/narendramodi/status/2073412135770808736#m
tweets.author: @sidhant

tweets.title: R to @NetramDefence: 7/7 For their extraordinary courage under extreme conditions, Grenadier Yogendra Singh Yadav was awarded the Param Vir Chakra, India's highest military decoration, and Lt. Balwan Singh was awarded the Maha Vir Chakra. 🇮🇳
tweets.content:
7/7

For their extraordinary courage under extreme conditions, Grenadier Yogendra Singh Yadav was awarded the Param Vir Chakra, India's highest military decoration, and Lt. Balwan Singh was awarded the Maha Vir Chakra. 🇮🇳
tweets.author: @NetramDefence

tweets.title: R to @NetramDefence: 6/7 His actions silenced the automatic fire, allowing the rest of the Ghatak Platoon to summit the cliff. Lt. Balwan Singh, despite being injured himself, led the remaining men to encircle the enemy positions. By the morning of July 4, the tricolor was hoisted atop Tiger Hill.
tweets.content:
6/7

His actions silenced the automatic fire, allowing the rest of the Ghatak Platoon to summit the cliff. Lt. Balwan Singh, despite being injured himself, led the remaining men to encircle the enemy positions. By the morning of July 4, the tricolor was hoisted atop Tiger Hill.
tweets.author: @NetramDefence

tweets.title: R to @NetramDefence: 5/7 Despite taking multiple bullets to his groin and shoulder, Yadav continued his ascent. Bleeding heavily, he crawled to the first enemy bunker, neutralized it with a grenade, and engaged in close combat, eliminating four enemy soldiers.
tweets.content:
5/7

Despite taking multiple bullets to his groin and shoulder, Yadav continued his ascent. Bleeding heavily, he crawled to the first enemy bunker, neutralized it with a grenade, and engaged in close combat, eliminating four enemy soldiers.
tweets.author: @NetramDefence

tweets.title: R to @NetramDefence: 4/7 A 19-year-old soldier, Grenadier Yogendra Singh Yadav, volunteered to lead the climb and fix the ropes for his platoon. Halfway up, the team was pinned down by heavy enemy machine-gun and rocket fire, which stalled the platoon's advance.
tweets.content:
4/7

A 19-year-old soldier, Grenadier Yogendra Singh Yadav, volunteered to lead the climb and fix the ropes for his platoon. Halfway up, the team was pinned down by heavy enemy machine-gun and rocket fire, which stalled the platoon's advance.
tweets.author: @NetramDefence

tweets.title: R to @NetramDefence: 3/7 The assault was spearheaded by the elite Ghatak Platoon of the 18 Grenadiers, commanded by then-Lieutenant Balwan Singh. Their objective was to ascend the steepest, 1,000-foot vertical cliff face of the mountain to catch the entrenched enemy by surprise.
tweets.content:
3/7

The assault was spearheaded by the elite Ghatak Platoon of the 18 Grenadiers, commanded by then-Lieutenant Balwan Singh. Their objective was to ascend the steepest, 1,000-foot vertical cliff face of the mountain to catch the entrenched enemy by surprise.
tweets.author: @NetramDefence

tweets.title: R to @NetramDefence: 2/7 The mission to retake the peak was assigned to the 18 Grenadiers, supported by the 8 Sikh Regiment and heavy artillery. Launched on the night of July 3, the operation required troops to scale sheer, vertical ice cliffs under the cover of darkness and sub-zero temperatures.
tweets.content:
2/7

The mission to retake the peak was assigned to the 18 Grenadiers, supported by the 8 Sikh Regiment and heavy artillery. Launched on the night of July 3, the operation required troops to scale sheer, vertical ice cliffs under the cover of darkness and sub-zero temperatures.
tweets.author: @NetramDefence

tweets.title: THREAD🧵 Kargil War 1999: Capturing Tiger Hill On July 4, 1999, the Indian Army captured Tiger Hill, marking a decisive turning point in the Kargil War. Rising 16,700 feet above the Mushkoh Valley, the peak gave enemy forces a dangerous line of sight over National Highway 1. Here is the factual account of the battle. @adgpi @NorthernComd_IA @ChinarcorpsIA @indiannavy @SpokespersonMoD @DefenceMinIndia @IAF_MCC @westerncomd_IA @suryacommand @IaSouthern @easterncomd @DefenceMinIndia @rajnathsingh 1/7
tweets.content:
THREAD🧵

Kargil War 1999: Capturing Tiger Hill

On July 4, 1999, the Indian Army captured Tiger Hill, marking a decisive turning point in the Kargil War. Rising 16,700 feet above the Mushkoh Valley, the peak gave enemy forces a dangerous line of sight over National Highway 1. Here is the factual account of the battle.

tweets.title: BREAKING: 50 Bangladeshi Nationals Sent to West Bengal from Tamilnadu for Deportation Authorities have transferred 50 Bangladeshi nationals from Tamil Nadu to West Bengal to facilitate their repatriation to Bangladesh. The group, 44 men and 6 women, had been lodged at a special detention camp in Salem district of Tamilnadu. They were escorted under tight security to Tiruchirappalli before being sent onward by train.
tweets.content:
BREAKING: 50 Bangladeshi Nationals Sent to West Bengal from Tamilnadu for Deportation

Authorities have transferred 50 Bangladeshi nationals from Tamil Nadu to West Bengal to facilitate their repatriation to Bangladesh.

The group, 44 men and 6 women, had been lodged at a special detention camp in Salem district of Tamilnadu. They were escorted under tight security to Tiruchirappalli before being sent onward by train.

tweets.title: UK Commits £8.6 Billion to Sixth-Generation GCAP Fighter Programme The United Kingdom has approved £8.6 billion ($11.4 billion) in funding over the next four years for the Global Combat Air Programme (GCAP), keeping development on track for service entry by 2035. The funding will enable the next major development contract to be awarded to Edgewing ahead of the Farnborough International Airshow. GCAP is a joint programme involving the United Kingdom, Italy, and Japan to develop a sixth-generation fighter featuring advanced sensors, networking capabilities, and next-generation combat technologies.
tweets.content:
UK Commits £8.6 Billion to Sixth-Generation GCAP Fighter Programme

The United Kingdom has approved £8.6 billion ($11.4 billion) in funding over the next four years for the Global Combat Air Programme (GCAP), keeping development on track for service entry by 2035.

The funding will enable the next major development contract to be awarded to Edgewing ahead of the Farnborough International Airshow.

GCAP is a joint programme involving the United Kingdom, Italy, and Japan to develop a sixth-generation fighter featuring advanced sensors, networking capabilities, and next-generation combat technologies.

tweets.title: BREAKING: India Designates 23 Pakistan-Based Terror Operatives Under UAPA The Ministry of Home Affairs (MHA) has designated 23 Pakistan-based operatives linked to UN-designated terrorist organisations, including Jaish-e-Mohammed (JeM) and Lashkar-e-Taiba (LeT), as individual terrorists under the Unlawful Activities (Prevention) Act (UAPA). The notifications cite their involvement in: - Cross-border infiltration into Jammu & Kashmir. - Recruitment, training, and financing of terrorists. - Arms and drone-based supply networks. - Major terror attacks, including the Nagrota (2016) and Sunjwan (2022) attacks This enables India to formally list these individuals as terrorists under domestic law.
tweets.content:
BREAKING: India Designates 23 Pakistan-Based Terror Operatives Under UAPA

The Ministry of Home Affairs (MHA) has designated 23 Pakistan-based operatives linked to UN-designated terrorist organisations, including Jaish-e-Mohammed (JeM) and Lashkar-e-Taiba (LeT), as individual terrorists under the Unlawful Activities (Prevention) Act (UAPA).

The notifications cite their involvement in:

- Cross-border infiltration into Jammu & Kashmir.
- Recruitment, training, and financing of terrorists.
- Arms and drone-based supply networks.
- Major terror attacks, including the Nagrota (2016) and Sunjwan (2022) attacks

This enables India to formally list these individuals as terrorists under domestic law.

tweets.title: I love the Unicorn radar on the Mogami class, and I’m sure future Indian warships adopting that antenna architecture will significantly reduce RCS. But at a time when warships are becoming increasingly vulnerable to UAVs, do we really want a single point of failure? Maybe it’s a weird thought, but I think it’s worth discussing.
tweets.content:
I love the Unicorn radar on the Mogami class, and I’m sure future Indian warships adopting that antenna architecture will significantly reduce RCS.

But at a time when warships are becoming increasingly vulnerable to UAVs, do we really want a single point of failure?

Maybe it’s a weird thought, but I think it’s worth discussing.

tweets.title: Daily US-Israel-Iran War Brief: 3rd Jul 2026: - We will be posting daily war updates on the ongoing war in Middle East - ⁠Follow us for daily Brief ● In response to Hezbollah’s repeated ceasefire violations, ~10 Hezbollah infrastructure sites in southern Lebanon, which were used to advance attacks against IDF soldiers, were struck. ● Senior foreign delegations from multiple nations, alongside Taliban and Hezbollah representatives, arrived in Tehran to pay their respects to Iran's late Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei. ● Iran began talks with Japanese companies to resume oil sales under a US sanctions waiver. ● Iran will not permit any US interference in the Strait of Hormuz, Parliament Speaker Ghalibaf told a senior Chinese official. ● NATO leaders are expected to declare that Iran must never develop a nuclear weapon and will call on Tehran to fully respect freedom of navigation in the Strait of Hormuz. END OF THE BRIEF
tweets.content:
Daily US-Israel-Iran War Brief:
3rd Jul 2026:

- We will be posting daily war updates on the ongoing war in Middle East
- ⁠Follow us for daily Brief

● In response to Hezbollah’s repeated ceasefire violations, ~10 Hezbollah infrastructure sites in southern Lebanon, which were used to advance attacks against IDF soldiers, were struck.

● Senior foreign delegations from multiple nations, alongside Taliban and Hezbollah representatives, arrived in Tehran to pay their respects to Iran's late Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei.

● Iran began talks with Japanese companies to resume oil sales under a US sanctions waiver.

● Iran will not permit any US interference in the Strait of Hormuz, Parliament Speaker Ghalibaf told a senior Chinese official.

● NATO leaders are expected to declare that Iran must never develop a nuclear weapon and will call on Tehran to fully respect freedom of navigation in the Strait of Hormuz.

END OF THE BRIEF

Netram Defence Review (@NetramDefence)

Daily US-Israel-Iran War Brief:
2nd Jul 2026:

- We will be posting daily war updates on the ongoing war in Middle East
- ⁠Follow us for daily Brief

● 5 VLCC supertankers, carrying a total of 10 million barrels of Saudi crude loaded from Ras Tanura, exited the Strait of Hormuz.

● Indirect US-Iran talks in Doha concluded without major breakthrough. Both sides agreed to continue negotiations on Strait of Hormuz navigation, frozen funds, and communication channels.

● The IDF eliminated a Hezbollah terrorist who exited one of the access shafts of the underground terror infrastructure at the Ali al-Taher Ridge, within the Security Zone.

● Iran's Parliament Speaker Ghalibaf reported that IAEA inspectors have been given access to nuclear sites damaged in US-Israeli strikes. He stated that Iranian law permits inspections at only two facilities: the Bushehr power plant and the Tehran reactor.

● Countries that took an inappropriate position on the U.S. and Israeli strikes were not invited to Ali Khamenei's funeral, according to Iranian Foreign Ministry spokesman Esmail Baghaei.

END OF THE BRIEF

tweets.title: There are no saints here : not even @ShivAroor bhai. 😂 and I understand his pressure. You just cannot ask them difficult questions else they won’t come (I tried it and I know - some of them in-fact asked questions in advance) I’d genuinely love to ask: what aircraft manufacturer anywhere in the world would happily accept an order split across three different production standards? 16 IOC fighters 16 FOC fighters 8 trainers Most OEMs would probably point to the exit. 😂and say that rectangular thing is door. That said, HAL isn’t beyond criticism either. Look at the timelines: 31 Mar 2006: Order for 16 IOC fighters + 4 trainers 23 Dec 2010: Order for 16 FOC fighters + 4 trainers 30 Sep 2014: First production IOC aircraft (SP-1) flies : 8 years after the first order 17 Mar 2020: First production FOC aircraft (SP-21) flies : nearly 10 years after the FOC order Then, after all that, the 83 Tejas Mk1A order received CCS approval only on 13 Jan 2021. The Tejas story has many villains. Procurement decisions, changing configurations, certification timelines, production bottlenecks, and industrial capacity all contributed. Blaming only the IAF or only HAL misses the bigger picture.
tweets.content:
There are no saints here : not even @ShivAroor bhai. 😂 and I understand his pressure. You just cannot ask them difficult questions else they won’t come (I tried it and I know - some of them in-fact asked questions in advance)

I’d genuinely love to ask: what aircraft manufacturer anywhere in the world would happily accept an order split across three different production standards?

16 IOC fighters
16 FOC fighters
8 trainers

Most OEMs would probably point to the exit. 😂and say that rectangular thing is door.

That said, HAL isn’t beyond criticism either. Look at the timelines:

31 Mar 2006: Order for 16 IOC fighters + 4 trainers
23 Dec 2010: Order for 16 FOC fighters + 4 trainers
30 Sep 2014: First production IOC aircraft (SP-1) flies : 8 years after the first order
17 Mar 2020: First production FOC aircraft (SP-21) flies : nearly 10 years after the FOC order

Then, after all that, the 83 Tejas Mk1A order received CCS approval only on 13 Jan 2021.

The Tejas story has many villains. Procurement decisions, changing configurations, certification timelines, production bottlenecks, and industrial capacity all contributed. Blaming only the IAF or only HAL misses the bigger picture.

ghatak (@Neetivaan)

Ex- IAF Chief AVM BS Dhanoa says, In 2019, Mig-21 was better than LCA Tejas

- Ctrl Alt Defence


Video

— https://nitter.net/Neetivaan/status/2073000517933166949#m

tweets.title: BREAKING: DAC Clears ₹52,000 Crore Defence Modernisation Package The Defence Acquisition Council (DAC), chaired by Defence Minister Rajnath Singh, has approved capital acquisition proposals worth ₹52,000 crore to strengthen the combat capabilities of India's Armed Forces. Indian Army • AKASH TARANG anti-UAV electronic warfare system • MPATGM anti-tank missiles • MRSAM air defence system • V-SHORADS air defence missiles • Active Protection System for tanks • Jet-based kamikaze drone system Indian Navy • Multi Influence Ground Mines (MIGM) • Naval Shipborne Unmanned Aerial System (NSUAS) • Land-Based Testing Facility for electric propulsion systems Indian Air Force • Fixed-Wing High Altitude Pseudo Satellite (FW-HAPS) for persistent ISR, telecommunications and remote sensing. The approvals are aimed at enhancing India's air defence, anti-drone capabilities, armoured survivability, naval warfare, and long-endurance surveillance capabilities.
tweets.content:
BREAKING: DAC Clears ₹52,000 Crore Defence Modernisation Package

The Defence Acquisition Council (DAC), chaired by Defence Minister Rajnath Singh, has approved capital acquisition proposals worth ₹52,000 crore to strengthen the combat capabilities of India's Armed Forces.

Indian Army
• AKASH TARANG anti-UAV electronic warfare system
• MPATGM anti-tank missiles
• MRSAM air defence system
• V-SHORADS air defence missiles
• Active Protection System for tanks
• Jet-based kamikaze drone system

Indian Navy
• Multi Influence Ground Mines (MIGM)
• Naval Shipborne Unmanned Aerial System (NSUAS)
• Land-Based Testing Facility for electric propulsion systems

Indian Air Force
• Fixed-Wing High Altitude Pseudo Satellite (FW-HAPS) for persistent ISR, telecommunications and remote sensing.

The approvals are aimed at enhancing India's air defence, anti-drone capabilities, armoured survivability, naval warfare, and long-endurance surveillance capabilities.

Netram Defence Review (@NetramDefence)

₹5,000 Crore Defence Procurement Set for DAC Approval

Defence Acquisition Council (DAC) is expected to clear major procurement proposals worth nearly ₹5,000 crore, aimed at boosting the armed forces' combat capabilities and indigenous defence production.

Key proposals include:

🔹 600 HAMMER precision-guided munitions for the IAF's Rafale, Tejas, and Navy's Rafale Marine fighters
🔹 100 MP-ATGM launchers, 2,300 indigenous anti-tank missiles, and 5 simulators for the Indian Army
🔹 Verba V-SHORADS for Army Air Defence
🔹 High-altitude pseudo satellites, kamikaze drones, drone detection systems, software-defined radios, and Scorpene submarine upgrades

The meeting marks the first DAC session with the new military leadership and is expected to further accelerate India's defence modernisation and Make in India initiatives.

— https://nitter.net/NetramDefence/status/2072979103519760837#m
tweets.author: @NetramDefence

tweets.title: LeT Reshuffles Leadership After Operation Sindoor Pakistan-based terror outfit Lashkar-e-Taiba (LeT) has reportedly carried out a major leadership reshuffle involving over 15 commanders across Pakistan's Punjab province following Operation Sindoor. According to open-source intelligence reports, the changes, ordered by Hafiz Saeed on 28 June, include the appointment of senior commanders to oversee training centres, recruitment networks, and infiltration routes. Key developments: • Haris Dar has been appointed to head Central Punjab, overseeing major LeT training facilities and infiltration corridors. • Imran Liaqat Bhatti, a senior arms trainer, has been moved to Kasur, which is reportedly emerging as a key training hub after the destruction of LeT's Muridke headquarters during Operation Sindoor. The reshuffle suggests LeT is attempting to reorganise and decentralise its infrastructure, dispersing training activities across multiple locations while reinforcing its command structure after recent operational setbacks.
tweets.content:
LeT Reshuffles Leadership After Operation Sindoor

Pakistan-based terror outfit Lashkar-e-Taiba (LeT) has reportedly carried out a major leadership reshuffle involving over 15 commanders across Pakistan's Punjab province following Operation Sindoor.

According to open-source intelligence reports, the changes, ordered by Hafiz Saeed on 28 June, include the appointment of senior commanders to oversee training centres, recruitment networks, and infiltration routes.

Key developments:
• Haris Dar has been appointed to head Central Punjab, overseeing major LeT training facilities and infiltration corridors.
• Imran Liaqat Bhatti, a senior arms trainer, has been moved to Kasur, which is reportedly emerging as a key training hub after the destruction of LeT's Muridke headquarters during Operation Sindoor.

The reshuffle suggests LeT is attempting to reorganise and decentralise its infrastructure, dispersing training activities across multiple locations while reinforcing its command structure after recent operational setbacks.
tweets.author: @NetramDefence

tweets.title: ₹5,000 Crore Defence Procurement Set for DAC Approval Defence Acquisition Council (DAC) is expected to clear major procurement proposals worth nearly ₹5,000 crore, aimed at boosting the armed forces' combat capabilities and indigenous defence production. Key proposals include: 🔹 600 HAMMER precision-guided munitions for the IAF's Rafale, Tejas, and Navy's Rafale Marine fighters 🔹 100 MP-ATGM launchers, 2,300 indigenous anti-tank missiles, and 5 simulators for the Indian Army 🔹 Verba V-SHORADS for Army Air Defence 🔹 High-altitude pseudo satellites, kamikaze drones, drone detection systems, software-defined radios, and Scorpene submarine upgrades The meeting marks the first DAC session with the new military leadership and is expected to further accelerate India's defence modernisation and Make in India initiatives.
tweets.content:
₹5,000 Crore Defence Procurement Set for DAC Approval

Defence Acquisition Council (DAC) is expected to clear major procurement proposals worth nearly ₹5,000 crore, aimed at boosting the armed forces' combat capabilities and indigenous defence production.

Key proposals include:

🔹 600 HAMMER precision-guided munitions for the IAF's Rafale, Tejas, and Navy's Rafale Marine fighters
🔹 100 MP-ATGM launchers, 2,300 indigenous anti-tank missiles, and 5 simulators for the Indian Army
🔹 Verba V-SHORADS for Army Air Defence
🔹 High-altitude pseudo satellites, kamikaze drones, drone detection systems, software-defined radios, and Scorpene submarine upgrades

The meeting marks the first DAC session with the new military leadership and is expected to further accelerate India's defence modernisation and Make in India initiatives.

Yes. Credit to them.” Then he cuts it straight: “Pakistan is a state teetering on the brink of failure.” “Diplomatic wins don’t feed people.” “Your problems are internal, economy mismanagement, and groups getting out of hand.” Final line: “If Pakistan didn’t have nuclear weapons, nobody would even worry.” VC @TheOGGodfeather
tweets.content:
Pak journalist Fatima Nazish: “Pakistan is becoming a global peace leader after US iran war, Where does Pakistan stand in the next 5 years?”

Singapore Ex-AmBilahari Kausikan:

“Pakistan was very agile in diplomacy. Yes. Credit to them.”

Then he cuts it straight:

“Pakistan is a state teetering on the brink of failure.”

“Diplomatic wins don’t feed people.”

“Your problems are internal, economy mismanagement, and groups getting out of hand.”

Final line:

“If Pakistan didn’t have nuclear weapons, nobody would even worry.”

tweets.content:
🎤 Pakistani journalist: "Pakistan borders Afghanistan, India, etc. Isn't its location the reason for all its problems?"

Singapore's Ex-amb Bilahari Kausikan: "In 1991, Pakistani hijackers hijacked a Singapore Airlines aircraft. They wanted to speak to Benazir Bhutto. We called her residence and explained the situation three times, warning that passengers could be killed if she did not speak to them. The reply? 'MADAM IS SLEEPING, SHE CANNOT BE DISTURBED'

He said the aircraft was then stormed. The hijackers were killed. The mystery died with them.

His conclusion: "Stop blaming geography. Pakistan's biggest problem is Pakistan"

tweets.title: Iran says US military bases in Gulf fuel insecurity, warns of more retaliation if attacks continue Iran has told the UN Security Council that U.S. military bases in the Gulf are a source of regional insecurity, warning that further attacks on Iran will be met with additional retaliation. Iran’s UN envoy, Amir-Saeid Iravani, also criticized the United Nations Security Council, accusing it of failing to uphold international law and saying its inaction has encouraged further military action against Iran.
tweets.content:
Iran says US military bases in Gulf fuel insecurity, warns of more retaliation if attacks continue

Iran has told the UN Security Council that U.S. military bases in the Gulf are a source of regional insecurity, warning that further attacks on Iran will be met with additional retaliation.

Iran’s UN envoy, Amir-Saeid Iravani, also criticized the United Nations Security Council, accusing it of failing to uphold international law and saying its inaction has encouraged further military action against Iran.

tweets.title: RT by @NetramDefence: INDUS WATERS TREATY: INDIA REJECTS HAGUE RULING India has rejected the Hague-based Court of Arbitration's ruling on the Indus Waters Treaty, maintaining that the court lacks jurisdiction because the treaty mandates a sequential dispute resolution process, starting with the Permanent Indus Commission, followed by a Neutral Expert, and only then arbitration. New Delhi also reiterated that the Indus Waters Treaty remains in abeyance following the Pahalgam terrorist attack and has asserted its right to fully utilize the waters allocated to India under the treaty. India continues to advocate a comprehensive review and modification of the 1960 treaty in light of current realities.
tweets.content:
INDUS WATERS TREATY: INDIA REJECTS HAGUE RULING

India has rejected the Hague-based Court of Arbitration's ruling on the Indus Waters Treaty, maintaining that the court lacks jurisdiction because the treaty mandates a sequential dispute resolution process, starting with the Permanent Indus Commission, followed by a Neutral Expert, and only then arbitration.

New Delhi also reiterated that the Indus Waters Treaty remains in abeyance following the Pahalgam terrorist attack and has asserted its right to fully utilize the waters allocated to India under the treaty.

India continues to advocate a comprehensive review and modification of the 1960 treaty in light of current realities.
tweets.author: @NetramDefence
